events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario: PVC flue slope back-pitched on a condensing furnace. This is a non-compliant and installed-defective condition. Back-pitched flue can trap condensate, causing flue blockage, spillage of combustion products including CO, which is a serious hazard. Need to fail, high or critical risk. Immediate controls: shut down furnace, correct slope per OEM/code (typically 1/4" per foot upward toward vent terminal or as specified), ensure condensate drainage, verify no blockage, test combustion/CO after repair.

Let me produce JSON.

"PVC condensing flue is back-pitched, allowing condensate to pool in the vent",
"Trapped condensate can block flue flow and cause combustion product spillage/CO entry",
"Vent slope does not meet IRC M1801/NFPA 54 requirements for condensing appliance venting"
],
"actions": [
"Shut down and disable the furnace until venting is corrected",
"Re-pitch PVC flue to required slope per OEM and code, with condensate draining back to the furnace trap or to an approved drain",
"Verify all joints, hangers, and supports maintain correct slope with no sags",
"Clear any standing condensate and inspect for blockage or joint failure",
"After repair, perform combustion analysis and confirm draft/vent operation before return to service"
],
"rationale": "A back-pitched condensing PVC flue traps acidic condensate, can obstruct venting, and risks spillage of flue gases including carbon monoxide. This is an unsafe, non-compliant installation requiring immediate shutdown and correction.",
"references": ["IRC M1801", "NFPA 54"]
}
